Class Spell

java.lang.Object
spell.Spell
Direct Known Subclasses:
FireBall, FrostBite, HealingBase, XSnap
public abstract class Spell extends Object
The Spell class serves as the abstract base for all magical abilities in the game. It defines the core attributes and the contract for execution through the cast method. * Spells are designed to be triggered by the player, typically resulting in area-of-effect (AoE) damage, debuffs, or structural repairs. By extending this class, specific spell types can implement unique behaviors while sharing common properties like range and damage.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    protected int
    damage
    The primary magnitude of the spell's effect, often representing health reduction.
    protected String
    name
    The display name of the spell.
    protected double
    range
    The radius or reach within which the spell's effect is active.
    protected String
    special
    A description or identifier for unique attributes of the spell (e.g., "Slow", "Heal").

    • name

      protected String name
      The display name of the spell.

    • damage

      protected int damage
      The primary magnitude of the spell's effect, often representing health reduction.

    • range

      protected double range
      The radius or reach within which the spell's effect is active.

    • special

      protected String special
      A description or identifier for unique attributes of the spell (e.g., "Slow", "Heal").

  • Constructor Details

    • Spell

      public Spell()

  • Method Details

    • cast

      public abstract void cast(double x, double y)
      Executes the spell's unique logic at the specified spatial coordinates. * Subclasses must implement this method to define how the spell interacts with the game environment (e.g., damaging enemies or healing the base).
      Parameters:
      x - The horizontal coordinate where the spell is cast.
      y - The vertical coordinate where the spell is cast.

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Returns:
      The name of the spell.

    • getDamage

      public int getDamage()
      Returns:
      The damage or primary intensity of the spell.

    • getRange

      public double getRange()
      Returns:
      The operational range of the spell.

    • getSpecial

      public String getSpecial()
      Returns:
      The special effect string associated with the spell.
